note: Answer key for the enterprise reasoning benchmark. For STRUCTURAL questions the graph proof is authoritative; 'evidence' and 'limitations' are required so the answer never overclaims. For ADVISORY questions, every entity in the matching scenario question's requiredEntities must exist in the fixture, and controls+risks+evidence+limitations must be supplied — these are design/consultant reasoning, NOT graph-executed and NOT vendor-certified.

controls: maker/checker enforced inside the procedure (MakerUser <> CheckerUser)
risks: dynamic SQL that names the proc indirectly would not resolve
limitations: locates the symbol; does not execute or approve anything

risks: reflection/dynamic dispatch could hide a caller
limitations: locates the symbol; no real incident is resolved

risks: a discount applied without the approval row would bypass control
limitations: graph shows the code paths; it does not certify a Dynamics/Salesforce control framework

controls: read-only assembly; per-project access still enforced by the platform
risks: fields added later must be wired into the 360 read
limitations: lists the tables the read touches; not a real customer dataset

controls: review every impacted service before a Customer schema change
risks: dynamic/ORM-generated SQL is not in the edge set, so impact is a floor not a ceiling
limitations: blast radius covers statically-named SQL only

risks: goods receipt before approval would break the control
limitations: pattern only; not a certified SAP/Oracle module map

risks: a GL posting-format change ripples into every finance report
limitations: no accounting-standard (GAAP/IFRS) guarantee

controls: stock movements are the single source for inventory reporting
risks: a movement-schema change breaks downstream reports
limitations: not a certified BI connector

risks: scheduling a change before approval bypasses governance
limitations: not certified ServiceNow workflow conformance

risks: an Incident schema change affects lifecycle, SLA, and the ops dashboard
limitations: impact covers statically-named SQL only

risks: release without a clean screen or checker would breach control
limitations: not a certified core-banking product; no fraud-proof guarantee

controls: screening result recorded with a list version
risks: stale list version invalidates the screen
limitations: no real sanctions list; screening accuracy not claimed

controls: rejection-code mapping drives retry vs terminal handling
risks: a code-mapping change alters payment routing/retry behaviour
limitations: not a complete external-scheme rejection catalogue

controls: release goes through the procedure and updates the maker/checker log
risks: bypassing the proc would skip the control
limitations: no settlement-finality guarantee

controls: approval row required before the discount is applied
risks: self-approval if segregation is not enforced
discount applied without an approval record
limitations: advisory control design; not a certified Dynamics/Salesforce control framework and not a fraud guarantee

controls: validate email on write before enforcing NOT NULL
risks: existing NULL emails break inserts/updates
the customer-360 read assumes the column is present
limitations: advisory impact; the migration was not executed here

risks: a valuation-method change shifts GL value and finance reporting
limitations: advisory; no accounting-standard correctness guarantee

controls: invoice posts to GL via the central procedure
risks: order without invoice, or invoice without GL posting, breaks the cash cycle
limitations: advisory module flow; not a certified ERP map

risks: receiving against an unapproved PO
limitations: advisory module flow; not a certified ERP map

risks: resolving without an audit entry loses traceability
limitations: advisory lifecycle; not certified ITIL conformance

risks: unapproved change reaching production
limitations: advisory; not certified ServiceNow change workflow

controls: single dashboard surfaces incidents, SLA breaches, and the payment pipeline
risks: stale snapshot misleads the manager
limitations: advisory; not a certified BI tool integration

controls: actor, action, and timestamp captured for every resolution
risks: missing audit rows undermine post-incident review
limitations: advisory; not a tamper-proof legal record

controls: segregation of duties at each stage
risks: release before a clean screen
maker == checker
limitations: advisory flow; not certified core-banking compliance and not fraud-proof

risks: self-release
release with a sanctions hit
limitations: advisory; no regulatory sign-off and no guaranteed AML detection

controls: list version recorded with each screen
risks: a rule/list change requires re-screening in-flight payments
limitations: advisory; no real or current sanctions list is shipped

controls: daily snapshot of open incidents, SLA breaches, payment pipeline
risks: acting on a stale or missing snapshot
limitations: advisory; not a live production feed

controls: maker, checker, approver, and stage recorded per payment with timestamps
risks: incomplete log undermines the segregation proof
limitations: advisory; not legal proof of compliance

controls: review every file in the impact set before migrating a shared table
risks: dynamic SQL means the impact set is a floor, so add manual review
limitations: advisory; driven by the structural impact graph, which excludes dynamic SQL

risks: an unreviewed patch touching a shared table could change payment behaviour
limitations: advisory; cross-links the ControlledExecutor proof, which ran on a synthetic workspace, not a real repo

risks: approving a change without the gates risks a regression or a page hang
limitations: advisory; gates are real (verify-poc/ui-smoke/security-audit) but not externally certified
